reasoning: The base rate of Republika Srpska actually scheduling a concrete date for an independence referendum is extremely low, despite frequent threats by Milorad Dodik to do so. A draft secessionist constitution was passed in March 2025, and as of late April 2025, there is debate about holding a public referendum to adopt it due to the lack of a two-thirds majority in the National Assembly. While Dodik suggested January 9 as a potential date for this constitutional referendum, it has not been officially scheduled or announced by an authorized entity. Furthermore, a referendum to adopt a constitution might not strictly satisfy the criteria of an 'independence referendum,' and Dodik has a history of retracting or delaying these votes under international and legal pressure. Given that only about 60 days remain until the July 1, 2025 deadline, and no formal procedures have been finalized to legally call this referendum, the likelihood of an official scheduling event happening in this narrowed window is very low.
